title: SRS airbag system post-crash activation inspection and replacement
section: Driver assistance SRS airbags seat belts

blocks.text: SRS airbag system post-crash activation inspection and replacement

items: Before repairing or inspecting the SRS, Check DTC in HDS. For DTCs, refer to Failure Diagnosis by DTC.

blocks.text: 1. SRS airbag system after collision activation Inspect and replace
Parts damaged during airbag deployment must be replaced, and the following replacement parts must be replaced even if they are not visually damaged.
When replacing the seat belt pretensioner, check for deformation of the seat belt mounting area and make the necessary modifications if deformation is found.
Seat belt Parts to be replaced when the pretensioner is deployed: 1.

blocks.text: 2. SRS airbag system After collision activation Interior parts Inspection

Other interior parts should be inspected and replaced if deformed or damaged. However, be sure to replace the clips and upper clips (A) of each pillar garnish even if they appear normal in appearance after the side curtain airbags are deployed.
After completing the work, connect the cable to the 12V battery minus (-) terminal, turn the ignition to the ON mode, and confirm that the SRS warning light comes on for 6 seconds and then goes off.

If the overlap between the roof lining and each pillar garnish (front pillar, center pillar upper and quarter pillar) is too large, it may result in abnormal deployment when the side curtain airbags are triggered. If the lap allowance of the front pillar, center pillar upper or quarter pillar exceeds 8mm, trim the roof lining.
1. Adjustment of wrap allowance for roof lining/pillar garnish
Temporarily assemble the pillar garnish (B) to the roof lining (A). The figure shows the front pillar garnish.

items: The upper clip of the front pillar garnish is designed to be partially damaged when removed (not reusable), so it must not be attached when temporarily assembling.

blocks.text: Align the masking tape (A) with the edge of the pillar garnish (B) and apply it to the roof lining side (C).
Remove the pillar garnish and measure the lap allowance.
If the lap allowance is 8 mm or more, trim the roof lining so that the lap allowance is 8 + 0/-2 mm.
